symbols: grand paintings on the walls — the accumulation of culture and worldly prestige
blue-and-white porcelain vases — imported luxury and display of wealth
gilded furniture and ornamental columns — the splendor of earthly power and aristocratic privilege
red walls — opulence and grandeur of a palatial interior
empty room — the absence of inhabitants amid great material abundance
description: A watercolor painting of a lavish interior salon with deep red walls hung with large gilt-framed portrait paintings. The room features ornate architectural details including decorated cornices and a gilded column on the left. On the floor stand two large blue-and-white porcelain vases. A gilded console table supports a dark bust. A doorway at center opens to an adjoining room. Additional smaller paintings and decorative objects fill the wall space. The overall impression is of a wealthy aristocratic gallery or state room, rendered in loose, luminous watercolor washes.
